name: Klotho and follistatin plasmid gene-therapy registry is recruiting without posted results
summary: The klotho plus follistatin plasmid registry adds no-results human trial-watch context and raises combination-attribution and safety boundaries.
statement: ClinicalTrials.gov lists a recruiting single-group early phase 1 Minicircle study of injectable nonviral plasmid-delivered klotho and follistatin gene therapy in 30 estimated healthy adults aged 50 to 80 years, with no posted results in the June 4, 2026 API view.
time_horizon: Registry start date December 16, 2025, with estimated primary completion in June 2026
quantitative_note: Estimated enrollment is 30; primary outcomes include serum alpha-klotho, serum follistatin, and treatment-emergent adverse events, with secondary cognitive, quality-of-life, epigenetic-age, physical-performance, inflammatory, renal, phosphate-axis, metabolic, and myostatin-axis endpoints.
caveats: The trial is recruiting and has no posted results.
The design is single-group and non-placebo-controlled.
Combination delivery prevents attribution to klotho, follistatin, or nonspecific effects without stronger controls.
Short registry follow-up and broad endpoint selection cannot establish durable aging benefit before results are posted.
